question: Design a C++ class named `Book` with private member variables for `title` (string), `author` (string), and `isbn` (string). Provide public getter and setter methods for each attribute.

test_cases.expected:
Book 1 Details:
Title: The Great Gatsby
Author: F. Scott Fitzgerald
ISBN: 978-0743273565
---
Book 2 Details:
Title: 1984
Author: George Orwell
ISBN: 978-0451524935
---
Book 3 Details (Robustness Test - Empty/Whitespace Input):
Title: ''
Author: ' '
ISBN: ''
---
Book 4 Details (Robustness Test - Empty Constructor Input):
Title: ''
Author: ''
ISBN: ''
---

errors: Lack of input validation in setter methods and constructor allows for invalid object states (e.g., empty or whitespace-only ISBN, title, or author). A robust class would typically validate inputs (e.g., check for non-empty strings) or throw exceptions to prevent such states.
